sources.title: Lost Horizon (1937)
sources.description: Lost Horizon is a fantasy film directed by Frank Capra, released on March 2, 1937. It is based on the 1933 novel by James Hilton. The film features a group of British citizens, led by diplomat Robert Conway, who crash-land in the Himalayas and discover the utopian society of Shangri-La. The film explores themes of peace, brotherhood, and the search for an ideal society.

sources.title: Lost Horizon 1937 colorized (Ronald Colman)
sources.description: Escaping by plane from a war-torn city in China, a British diplomat and a small group of civilians crash land in the mountains of Tibet, where they are rescued by a group of monks who live in a mysterious, Eden-like valley called Shangri-la. It offers them a seductive escape from the outside world, where the clouds of World War II are gathering. Directed by Frank Capra.  Based on the novel by James Hilton. Ronald Colman as Robert Conway Jane Wyatt as Sondra Edward Everett Horton as Lovett John Howard as George Conway Thomas Mitchell as Barnard Margo as Maria Isabel Jewell as Gloria H.B. Warner as Chang Sam Jaffe as the High Lama Hugh Buckler Lord Gainsford If you have a request or a comment for me, please go to the Colorized Movies Forum at https://archive.org/details/colorized-movies?tab=forum I colorized this using AI software that doesn't always get the colors right, but I think it looks fairly natural. Hopefully a more professional job will be done someday.

sources.title: Lost Horizon (restored, colorized) (1937, fantasy, imdb score: 7.7)
sources.description: Lost Horizon is a 1933 novel by English writer James Hilton . The book was turned into a film, also called Lost Horizon , in 1937 by director Frank Capra and a lavish musical remake in 1973 by producer Ross Hunter with music by Burt Bacharach . It is best remembered as the origin of Shangri-La , a fictional utopian lamasery located high in the mountains of Tibet .

metadata.Plot: British diplomat Robert Conway and a small group of civilians crash land in the Himalayas, and are rescued by the people of the mysterious, Eden-like valley of Shangri-la. Protected by the mountains from the world outside, where the clouds of World War II are gathering, Shangri-la provides a seductive escape for the world-weary Conway.
metadata.Writer: Robert Riskin, James Hilton, Sidney Buchman
metadata.Actors: Ronald Colman, Jane Wyatt, Edward Everett Horton
